title: The Rhythm of Repair: Rebuilding Trust After Systemic Harm - My Framer Site
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeWebinarsThe Rhythm of Repair: Rebuilding Trust After Systemic HarmSharon MamoEducationShareRepairing trust is a rhythmic process that requires acknowledgment, consistency, and care. In this session, Sharon discusses how organisations can rebuild relationships after harm by fostering transparency, empathy, and shared accountability. Drawing on SAMMS’ cross-sector experiences, she explores how reflective communication transforms apology into sustained, restorative practice that re-establishes confidence between services and communities.UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →AllLeadershipCommunityEducationSystemsReflectionPolicyCollaborationResearchInnovationPractice45:43FeaturedReframing Complexity: Trauma-Informed Practice in Modern SystemsSharon MamoInnovation26:54FeaturedRestoring Stability: A Housing-First Approach to Complex CareMontana NicoloutsopoulosCollaboration33:47Building Connection in Remote PracticeSharon MamoCollaboration41:15Leading with Care: Trauma-Informed Leadership in ActionMontana NicoloutsopoulosReflection36:20The Power of Story: Reconnecting Through Narrative PracticeSharon MamoSystems58:32Mapping the Nervous System: Practical Neuro-Informed Tools for CliniciansSharon MamoCommunity43:21Reflective Supervision: Creating Safe Spaces for GrowthMontana Nicolou

body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeWebinarsInnovation at the Edges: New Models for Systemic ReformMontana NicoloutsopoulosInnovationShareSystemic change rarely originates at the centre—it begins at the edges, where curiosity meets courage. Montana presents contemporary examples of trauma-informed innovation emerging from collaborative partnerships and adaptive leadership. title: Restoring Stability: A Housing-First Approach to Complex Care - My Framer Site
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeWebinarsRestoring Stability: A Housing-First Approach to Complex CareMontana NicoloutsopoulosCollaborationShareThis webinar explores how trauma-informed frameworks reshape the housing landscape by focusing on connection over correction. SAMMS facilitators unpack the Housing-First approach, emphasising how stability and safety lay the foundation for long-term behavioural and emotional change. The discussion highlights the value of interagency collaboration, reflective supervision, and adaptive care models that prioritise human dignity within complex service systems.UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →UPCOMING MASTERCLASSSTAY IN THE LOOPBe the first to hear about our next masterclass.Drop your email and we'll let you know when registration opens for the next SAMMs webinar.Notify Me →AllLeadershipCommunityEducationSystemsReflectionPolicyCollaborationResearchInnovationPractice45:43FeaturedReframing Complexity: Trauma-Informed Practice in Modern SystemsSharon MamoInnovation33:47Building Connection in Remote PracticeSharon MamoCollaboration39:17The Rhythm of Repair: Rebuilding Trust After Systemic HarmSharon MamoEducation41:15Leading with Care: Trauma-Informed Leadership in ActionMontana NicoloutsopoulosReflection36:20The Power of Story: Reconnecting Through Narrative PracticeSharon MamoSystems58:32Mapping the Nervous System: Practical Neuro-Informed Tools for CliniciansSharon MamoCommunity43:21Reflective Supervision: Creating Safe Spaces for GrowthMontana Nico

title: Integrated Support for Complex and High-Risk Needs   - My Framer Site
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutImpact HubResourcesContact UsEmergencyServicesHomeAboutImpact HubResourcesFAQEmergencyContact UsStart for FreeIntegrated Support for Complex and High-Risk Needs 1 minuteTraining & PDA structured clinical intervention for a client with justice involvement, chronic substance use, and cognitive complexity — resulting in ADHD diagnosis, reduced offending, and supported disability pathways.Background The client presented with long-standing and complex needs, including a history of institutionalisation across the criminal justice system, out-of-home care, and psychiatric services. At referral, the client was experiencing ongoing substance use, cognitive concerns, and challenging behaviours that significantly impacted their engagement with employment services.They were frequently missing appointments, attending sessions while substance-affected, and had no active case management or coordinated support in place within the greater community. Employment was unlikely but there was no progress in obtaining documentation to support disability pathways.Our ApproachUpon intake, our qualified clinician completed a Risk, Need and Capacity assessment, with risks clearly identified and service delivery modified accordingly — for example, larger rooms and flexible engagement strategies where there was a risk of aggression. To build trust, case management was used to demonstrate immediate outcomes and actions, such as a dental referral.A Self-Sufficiency Matrix was used to identify the client's top three priority needs and assess their level of insight and self-awareness. Using the FISH model, foundational needs were addressed first to stabilise the client and reduce their crisis state.Upcoming legal matters were identified, including a significant risk of incarceration and no legal support. A referral was made to our collaborating lawyer and a support letter outlining the client's treatment plan was provided to assist with court proceedings.Harm-reduction strategies and psychoeduc

title: Australia’s first self-funded Complex Needs Clinical Service for Employment Services - My Framer Site
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutServicesImpact HubResourcesContact UsTerms of ServiceEmergencyMake A ReferralEmergencyAboutServicesImpact HubResourcesContact UsTerms of ServiceEmergencyMake A ReferralAustralia’s first self-funded Complex Needs Clinical Service for Employment Services4 minutesbyMontana NicoloutsopoulosMedia releaseEmployment services reformComplex needsAustralia’s first self-funded Complex Needs Clinical Service designed specifically for Employment Services participants opens tomorrow in Sunshine, marking a major milestone for long-term unemployed people navigating the increasingly complex Centrelink system.The SAMMs Complex Needs Clinic is a first-of-its-kind clinical service built for people facing entrenched, high-risk and long-term barriers including mental health challenges, addiction, criminal justice involvement, disability, chronic illness, trauma and severe social instability.Located at 1/5 Devonshire Road, Sunshine, the clinic launches in the same community where Sharon Mamo, CEO of Creating New Pathways and founder of the SAMMs model, once faced the very barriers she now specialises in addressing. For Ms Mamo, this opening is both a full-circle moment and a turning point in how complex unemployment is understood and supported in Victoria.Built by lived experience, proven through clinical designMs Mamo and her daughter, Montana Nicoloutsopoulos, a Mental Health Social Worker and 2025 Australian Social Worker of the Year nominee, self-funded the research, development and build of the SAMMs model. Their mission was to create a clinical service that acts quickly, intelligently and compassionately for people repeatedly failed by overstretched systems and reduced to tick-box compliance.These are individuals who often feel isolated, misunderstood and unable to advocate for themselves. Many struggle to meet constantly shifting Centrelink requirements without the support, regulatory capacity or clinical scaffolding needed to understand what is expected of them. SAMMs respond

title: The Partnerships That Built SAMMs - My Framer Site
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: AboutServicesImpact HubResourcesContact UsTerms of ServiceEmergencyMake A ReferralEmergencyAboutServicesImpact HubResourcesContact UsTerms of ServiceEmergencyMake A ReferralThe Partnerships That Built SAMMs4 minutesbySharon MamoClinical innovationEmployment services reformComplex needsWhy SAMMs ExistsSAMMs exists because people inside the system saw the cracks and had the courage to ask for something better. We were invited to step in where traditional counselling, activation and short-term interventions had already failed the people they were meant to help.The Early WISE Employment CollaborationIn the early years, WISE Employment engaged SAMMs CEO, Sharon Mamo, and Director, Montana Nicoloustopoulos, to assess and respond to a clear service gap within their ex-offender and complex-needs caseloads, an area both organisations recognised as critical to reform.At WISE’s request, Sharon and Montana provided extensive clinical consultation, program redevelopment, and workforce guidance to help their sites respond safely and effectively to participants facing entrenched psychosocial and neurological barriers.Building the FrameworkOver more than two years, Creating New Pathways self-funded and delivered thousands of professional hours to the sector, including clinical program design, case reviews, staff consultation, and system improvement.During this time, SAMMs developed a comprehensive model of care that integrated neuroscience, trauma-informed practice, and behavioural regulation principles into employment servicing.These activities informed the creation of the SAMMs Clinical Framework model owned and governed by Creating New Pathways, and now implemented nationally.What the Early Results DemonstratedThe early collaboration demonstrated the value of embedding clinicians within employment systems, turning engagement into stability, and crisis into capacity.Ongoing Employment PartnersWe also acknowledge our ongoing partners, SYC, APM, Octec, and Uniting Vic.Tas who conti
